headline: Why The Tesco (LSE:TSCO) Story Is Shifting After The New £4.80 Price Target

headline: JPMorgan Limits Private Credit Lending After Loan Markdowns
summary: The devalued loans are to software companies, the person added, a sector that’s been in the spotlight in recent weeks due to investor concern over the potential impact of artificial intelligence.  JPMorgan Chief Executive Officer Jamie Dimon told investors at the bank’s leveraged finance conference last week that it’s being more prudent in lending against software assets, according to the report.

headline: JPMorgan Restricts Private Credit Lending After Loan Markdowns
summary: The devalued loans are to software companies, the person added, some of the biggest borrowers fueling the growth of the private credit market.  A reduction in the value of those assets will curtail the amount the bank is able to lend to these funds, heaping further pressure on an industry already grappling with a succession of hefty withdrawals from retail investors, spooked by renewed scrutiny of underwriting standards and developments in AI.  Cliffwater LLC, for instance, became the latest private credit firm to face redemption requests in excess of 7% from its flagship fund, Bloomberg News reported on Tuesday, following similar demands from investors in funds managed by BlackRock Inc., Blackstone Inc. and Blue Owl Capital Inc.

headline: These 4 ETFs Generate $6,000 a Year in Monthly Income for Retirement Investors
summary: Four ETFs, each yielding above 6%, each paying monthly. On a $100,000 investment spread across these funds, the income math works out to more than $6,000 a year before taxes. That kind of cash flow is what income investors are hunting for, especially with the 10-year Treasury sitting at 4.13% and the Fed Funds Rate ... headline: JPMorgan Restricts Private Credit Lending After Markdowns
summary: A reduction in the value of those assets will curtail the amount the bank is able to lend to these funds, heaping further pressure on an industry already grappling with a series of hefty withdrawals from retail investors, spooked by renewed scrutiny of underwriting standards and developments in AI.  JPMorgan’s moves have only impacted a small cohort of its borrowers, the people familiar with the matter said, asking not to be named discussing non-public information.  A representative for JPMorgan declined to comment on the latest changes, which were first reported by the Financial Times.

content.title: JPMorgan Restricts Private Credit Lending After Markdowns

JPMorgan’s moves have only impacted a small cohort of its borrowers, the people familiar with the matter said, asking not to be named discussing non-public information.  A representative for JPMorgan declined to comment on the latest changes, which were first reported by the Financial Times.

headline: How Workday (WDAY) Story Is Shifting As AI Spend Rises And Growth Expectations Reset
summary: Workday’s fair value estimate in the updated model moved from US$231.17 to US$184.73, a cut of about 20% that resets expectations around the stock. This shift lines up with more cautious Street commentary after Q4 results, CEO changes and reduced long term targets, even as some analysts still point to AI, finance and HR automation as longer term positives. In the sections that follow, you will see what is driving this evolving narrative and how to keep track of it as new information comes...

headline: Treasury Prices Drop After Back-to-Back Weak Auctions
summary: The war in the Middle East, and its impact on a volatile stock market, should have created conditions for investors to run to Treasuries, a longtime haven.  Investors were awarded 4.217% during the 10-year note sale, which was 0.6 basis points above the yield seen before the bidding deadline.  Higher rates at auctions signal that demand is weak, as the Treasury has to entice investors to buy U.S. debt.

content.title: Treasury Prices Drop After Back-to-Back Weak Auctions
content.summary: The war in the Middle East, and its impact on a volatile stock market, should have created conditions for investors to run to Treasuries, a longtime haven.  Investors were awarded 4.217% during the 10-year note sale, which was 0.6 basis points above the yield seen before the bidding deadline.  Higher rates at auctions signal that demand is weak, as the Treasury has to entice investors to buy U.S. debt.
